outcome_contract.why: Work can be marked complete even if it's missing key sections like engineering coverage evidence or outcome statements. Without checking completeness automatically, gaps only surface when someone manually reviews.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Validator pass/fail results are captured in workflow telemetry so completeness gaps are visible in reports without manual review

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A — internal telemetry data pipeline, no UI
N/A — CLI and data records, no user-facing states
N/A — internal tooling reading local files
Required — this IS the observability improvement
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Validator outcomes become visible in telemetry reports

engineering_coverage.applicable: Required — TC contracts for new field, report, dedupe
Required — interface addition, schema doc bump
N/A — append-only JSONL, no hot path
N/A — additive optional field, old records unaffected

stage_payload.summary: Add a `deterministic_check_results` optional field to `WorkflowStepTelemetryRecord` that captures compact validator pass/fail summaries (`{ valid, error_count, warning_count }` per check). Extend the telemetry CLI with a `--check-result` flag to accept these results, and add a quality/completeness section to the report generator. Update the schema doc to v1.6.0. This follows the exact pattern established by C003 (`per_module_bytes`).
goals: Capture validator pass/fail results in telemetry records
Enable the report generator to surface quality/completeness data alongside existing size and token metrics
non_goals: Changing validator logic or pass/fail criteria
constraints: Backward-compatible with existing records (new field optional)
Must not break existing consumers (`summarizeWorkflowStepTelemetry`, `computePerModuleBreakdown`, ideas rollup)
assumptions: All validators produce JSON to stdout with typed result interfaces
selected_approach: Option A: Add an optional `deterministic_check_results` field with compact per-check summary objects to `WorkflowStepTelemetryRecord`. Add a `--check-result` CLI flag. Add quality section to report generator. Bump schema to 1.6.0.
Why planning is not reopening option selection:
Analysis compared 3 options against 5 explicit criteria; Option A won decisively on all high-priority criteria. No new evidence has emerged.

task_briefs.title: Add deterministic_check_results to record type, builder, CLI, and dedupe key
Add validator results to report generator and update schema doc

delivered_process_areas.Trigger: Agent completes DO stage, validators pass
delivered_process_areas.Delivered step-by-step flow: 1. Agent runs validators → gets JSON stdout. 2. Agent extracts `valid`, error count, warning count from each result. 3. Agent calls telemetry CLI with `--deterministic-check <name>` AND `--check-result <name>:<pass\
delivered_process_areas.Tasks / dependencies: fail>:<errors>:<warnings>` for each check. 4. CLI parses `--check-result` flags into `deterministic_check_results` field. 5. Record appended with both script names and results.

delivered_process_areas.Delivered step-by-step flow: 1. Report reads JSONL records. 2. For records with `deterministic_check_results`, aggregates pass/fail counts and error/warning totals per check name. 3. Markdown output includes `## Validator Results` section. 4. JSON output includes `validator_summary` in envelope.
delivered_process_areas.Unresolved issues / rollback seam: Must handle mixed records (some with results, some legacy without)
